mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate raises corporate discount rates and debt service costs, reducing present-value equity cash flows and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS increases month-over-month during a tightening cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 2000 thousand units indicate a contraction in residential investment and construction employment that spills over into aggregate demand, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances when HOUST is below 2000.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decline in retail sales and increase in the federal funds rate reveals a monetary-policy-induced demand contraction that amplifies equity downside risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening with consumer demand slowdown
failure_condition: The joint signal of declining RSAFS and rising FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 20-day returns with a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 5% during a late-cycle expansion reflects an overheated labor market that produces wage-price spirals and aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations or the Sharpe ratio of the short signal exceeds zero.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and operating leverage that lifts corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales translates directly into higher nominal corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month increases fail to coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate concurrent with rising payroll employment reflects a tightening cycle that lacks labor market distress, enabling deeper and more prolonged rate hikes that reduce equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening with resilient labor markets
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising FEDFUNDS and rising PAYEMS fails to produce negative 60-day returns, with positive returns occurring in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP intensifies inflationary pressure and forces Federal Reserve rate hikes, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth coincides with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 45% of observations over any 5-year rolling window.
